item.title: Explorations in Poetic Transcendence with Ruben
item.description:
Steve Ying-Chu Chen recently participated in an English Reading Club session hosted by Ruben, which embarked on a rigorous intellectual exploration of poetic form and its capacity to encapsulate the human condition—a domain often dismissed as mere abstraction, yet profoundly significant in its linguistic and cognitive complexity. The group began by redefining “poetry” not as a static arrangement of verse, but as a dynamic interplay of phonology, semantics, and emotional resonance.

Under Ruben’s astute moderation, the discussion transcended the pedestrian clichés of rhyming schemes or basic imagery. Instead, participants, including Steve, engaged with the foundational principles that govern “poetic economy”—the mechanism by which maximum affect is achieved through minimal, yet precise, linguistic input. Their analysis centered on the concept of “metaphorical salience,” dissecting how poets utilize cross-domain mapping to bridge the gap between tangible experience and metaphysical inquiry. They examined the dichotomy between “formalist precision,” which demands structural rigor, and “negative capability,” the capacity to remain in uncertainties and mysteries without an irritable reaching after fact and reason.

Furthermore, the attendees grappled with the sophisticated evolution of contemporary poetics, contrasting the traditional “mimetic” model (where poetry reflects external reality) with the “expressive” and “objective” models. This latter framework compellingly positions the poem as an autonomous object of inquiry, where the individual’s perception is both the architect and the subject of the literary experience. The group acknowledged that this paradigm shift is the conceptual progenitor of modern experimental verse and the reclamation of oral traditions in the digital age.

The discussions challenged the participants to synthesize these abstract models and apply them rigorously to their own cognitive frameworks, collectively exploring how poetry illuminates the nuances of human empathy, the limitations of literal language, and the enduring pursuit of aesthetic truth. It was a high-level discourse that successfully dissected the complex architecture of verbal art underpinning our shared cultural consciousness.
